danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy sprites: phantom ice-cream trucks, packs of nerds, giant hamsters, Bogeymen, man-eating mailboxes, armed chickens
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities that can damage player; threat increases with encounter frequency

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen boundary at edge of floating island platform
danger_signals.meaning: Falling hazard; forces player to locate elevator to return to current level

danger_signals.visual_form: Special wrapped present (visual appearance resets; question mark variant)
danger_signals.meaning: All previously identified presents lose their identification state; increases danger as harmful items become unpredictable again

danger_signals.visual_form: Wrapped present with specific visual wrapper design
danger_signals.meaning: Causes player to lose a life; negative power-up concealed until opened

safety_signals.visual_form: Wrapped presents with consistent visual appearance after first opening
safety_signals.meaning: Known beneficial power-ups; player can selectively open for gameplay advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Certain NPC enemies marked as non-hostile (e.g., carrot-costumed NPC)
safety_signals.meaning: Aid player through item identification service or other assistance; safe to approach

safety_signals.visual_form: Elevator structures connecting floating islands vertically
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation safety; allows escape from current level if cornered by enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Split-screen unites into single screen when both characters near each other
safety_signals.meaning: Co-op partnership active; shared resource effects and mutual support

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: ToeJam: gold medallion + backwards baseball cap; Earl: high-tops + oversized sunglasses
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien rappers from Funkotron; spacecraft wreckage debris scattered across Earth islands represents their faction's presence and goal

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse visual designs: ice-cream trucks, nerds, hamsters, Bogeymen, mailboxes, armed chickens—represents Earth's hostile and absurd nature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct enemy types; some marked as helpful vs. harmful through visual differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Populate all floating islands; represent Earth civilization as satirical threat to alien visitors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Reconstructed spacecraft; greeting by friends/family at escape
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final level environment depicted as purple landscape; represents alien cultural space distinct from Earth's multi-colored islands

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game / few spacecraft pieces collected
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple floating islands visible; dense enemy population; many unidentified wrapped presents
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higher encounter frequency with random enemies; unpredictable present effects increase danger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Identified presents cluster by wrapper color; elevator frequency increases accessibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains information advantage; can selectively open known beneficial items; Randomizer becomes greater threat as present identification accumulates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Higher concentration of identified safe presents; purple Funkotron landscape approaches as escape sequence nears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced enemy threat relative to player power-up access; final sequence shows characters crossing purple alien landscape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Purple landscape environment; friends and family present visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Non-interactive narrative sequence; establishes safe return to alien civilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen layout shifts: single screen when players near, split-screen when separated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cooperative dialogue and jokes reveal in two-player only; shared power-up effects when players proximate

environmental_storytelling.technique: Surreal enemy design as cultural satire
environmental_storytelling.example: Phantom ice-cream trucks, armed chickens, man-eating mailboxes, and nerds as hostile 'Earthlings' communicate world-state as absurdist commentary on 1990s American culture; enemies visually embody mundane Earth objects imbued with hostile intent

environmental_storytelling.technique: Floating island topology as planetary metaphor
environmental_storytelling.example: Disconnected islands floating in space represent Earth as hostile, fragmented world; elevator-based navigation emphasizes escape difficulty and planetary layering

environmental_storytelling.technique: Protagonist visual design as cultural appropriation parody
environmental_storytelling.example: ToeJam and Earl's 'over-the-top' 1990s urban fashion (medallion, backwards cap, high-tops, oversized sunglasses) establish them as alien outsiders parodying Earth culture they don't understand

environmental_storytelling.technique: Present wrapper color as information-encoding system
environmental_storytelling.example: Each wrapped present's visual appearance categorizes its contents after first identification; unknowable presents marked with question marks; system communicates player knowledge state and risk management

environmental_storytelling.technique: Funkotron ending environment as safe-space visual contrast
environmental_storytelling.example: Purple landscape and welcoming family/friends visually establish homeworld as opposite of Earth's hostile, colorful chaos; narrative conclusion uses environmental shift to communicate success and safety

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Near each other in two-player mode
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Screen unites; cooperative dialogue plays; shared present effects; visual and audio cues strengthen sense of partnership
Screen splits; characters operate independently; increase vulnerability and isolation communication
Antagonistic pursuit; varied attack patterns (tomato-throwing chickens, ice-cream truck movement, hamster aggression) communicate distinct threat profiles through visual behavior
Offers present identification service for fee; visual costume design signals non-hostile intent despite Earthling category
Visual wrapper appearance unknown; question mark variant signals complete uncertainty; player must decide risk-reward
All subsequent presents with matching wrapper show identified contents; visual consistency enables player strategy; information state visually encoded
All present identifications reset; forces re-exploration and risk re-evaluation; game difficulty recalibrates through present unpredictability

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Player character falling from island edge
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Falls to island below; must relocate elevator for vertical recovery; visual consequence of navigation error

notes: ToeJam & Earl uses visual and behavioral manifestation to communicate its satirical world-state: Earth as hostile, absurdist danger contrasted with Funkotron as safe alien home. The present-identification system is core to manifestation design—unknown visual wrappers encode player uncertainty and risk management, while consistent color/appearance after opening creates a learnable, information-dense environment. Enemy diversity (ice-cream trucks, Bogeymen, armed chickens) satirizes American culture through hostile manifestation. The 3/4 perspective and floating island topology visually emphasize player alienation and planetary strangeness. Screen-splitting in co-op mode dynamically manifests player separation as mechanical and visual isolation. The purple Funkotron ending uses environmental color shift to signal safety and narrative resolution.
